title: Discipline
tagline: Nine tracks. Cork hip-hop with collaborators across two continents — the LP that pulled together two years of late-night sessions and verses traded across timezones.
credits: Featuring Shanakee, Farid Williams, Otaku Black, Goldie Bron$on, Haneli, SaturnAstrid, Jimmy Cena, and JusMe (scratch). Influences: Massive Attack's Mezzanine, Stupeflip, XXV-era compilations.

sections.title: Why This Album Exists
sections.body: Discipline is the LP that pulled together two years of late-night sessions and verses traded across timezones. Built around heavyweight features — Shanakee and Farid Williams thread through almost every track — it's also the record where the remix of one cut hit #16 on Apple Music Ireland's Hip-Hop/Rap Albums chart.

sections.title: Behind the Production
sections.body: Influences sit on the record like fingerprints: the dense low-end of Massive Attack's Mezzanine, the wired-up chaos of Stupeflip, the wide-eyed scope of XXV-era compilations. Tracked between Cork sessions and remote stems from collaborators. Mixed for headphones first, then made to survive the car.

sections.title: Credits & Features
sections.body: Featuring Shanakee, Farid Williams, Otaku Black, Goldie Bron$on, Haneli, SaturnAstrid, Jimmy Cena, JusMe (scratch). Special thanks to everyone who showed up to the booth and to the listeners who pushed the remix into the IE chart.

tracks.title: I hear the rain X Haneli
The Moocher X Goldie Bron$on X Jack Eagan
Ooh La La La! (feat. Shanakee & Farid Williams)
The Kraken X Otaku Black X Jimmy Cena
Ça Me Saoule (feat. Shanakee & Farid Williams)

title: Folks Now! / Minnie
tagline: Two-track single — the Discipline cuts that wouldn't sit still, finally on their own terms.
credits: Minnie lands solo here after appearing in its X SaturnAstrid form on Discipline. Folks Now! brings Jaws and Farid Williams back into the room.

sections.title: About These Two
sections.body: 'Minnie' was always going to be the one people came back to — featured on Discipline in its X SaturnAstrid form, here it lands solo. 'Folks Now!' brings Jaws and Farid Williams back into the room. Two tracks, two energies.

title: No Eyes
tagline: Six minutes. One track. No Eyes. A long-form single, written and recorded in the gap between Discipline and the next thing.
credits: A long-form single — six minutes is a deliberate choice; the song earns it.

sections.title: About 'No Eyes'
sections.body: A long-form single, written and recorded in the gap between Discipline and the next thing. Six minutes is a deliberate choice — the song earns it.

title: Wanna Be Seen
tagline: Cork meets Cork. Poolpat × Jaws, two and a half minutes flat — the kind of single you stick on at the start of a night.
credits: Quick, direct, no chorus tax. Cork-on-Cork collaboration with Jaws.

sections.title: About 'Wanna Be Seen'
sections.body: Quick, direct, no chorus tax. The kind of single you stick on at the start of a night.

title: Get It Out
tagline: The opening shot. December 2023 — the single that set the tone for what would become Discipline.
credits: First Poolpat × Farid Williams release. Three minutes that previewed the chemistry the LP would lean on.

sections.title: About 'Get It Out'
sections.body: First Poolpat × Farid Williams release. Three minutes that previewed the chemistry the LP would lean on.

title: Jimmy Cena × Shanakee (Remix by Poolpat)
tagline: Poolpat remixes the Jimmy Cena × Shanakee sessions — five tracks reworked from the ground up.
credits: Full remix production by Poolpat on the Jimmy Cena × Shanakee EP. Features Djar One, Bubba Shakespeare, Emi J, Farid William, and Jaws.

sections.title: About This Remix EP
sections.body: Full remix production by Poolpat on the Jimmy Cena × Shanakee EP. Charted #16 Hip-Hop/Rap and #120 All Albums on Apple Music Ireland.

tracks.title: Tapis Rouge (feat. Djar One, Bubba Shakespeare & Emi J) [Remix]
J'peux pas y a le match (feat. Djar One & Farid William) [Remix]

title: (Unfinished) Legacy
tagline: Jack Egan's release featuring Poolpat across three tracks — including a remastered cut of 'The Moocher' and two Irish-language instrumentals.
credits: Appears-on release by Jack Egan. Three tracks featuring Poolpat: a remastered Moocher with Goldie Bron$on, plus two Irish-language instrumentals.

sections.title: About '(Unfinished) Legacy'
sections.body: Jack Egan's release featuring Poolpat across three tracks: a remastered Moocher with Goldie Bron$on, plus two Irish-language instrumentals (Cara Mo Bhean Chéile, Teacht Le Chéile).
